title: Elon Musk's Starlink blocked from operating in Namibia
content: Namibia's regulator noted that Starlink's subsidiary is not locally owned.

originalTitle: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
title: Big News from Angola: Friends Go to Court!
content:
Hello, Super Friends! Your Africa News Reporter is Here!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some exciting news from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we are going to talk about a beautiful country called Angola. It's a very special place, just like where many of you live! Angola is a big country in the southern part of Africa. It has sunny beaches, tall trees, and many happy people. The capital city of Angola is called Luanda. Can you say Luanda? Great job!

What's Happening in Angola?

Something important is happening in Angola. Some grown-ups are going to a special place called a court. A court is like a big classroom for grown-ups when they have a big question or a disagreement that needs to be solved. They need a judge, who is like a super-smart teacher, to help them understand what happened and what is fair. So, what is the big question? Well, some people are saying that a group of grown-ups tried to start some special kinds of big meetings. These meetings are called 'protests'.

What is a Protest?

Imagine you and your friends really, really want to play outside, but maybe the grown-ups say it's not a good time. Or maybe you all feel very strongly about something. If many, many people feel the same way about something important in their town or country, they might want to tell everyone how they feel. They might gather together in a special place, hold signs, or sing songs to share their message. This is called a protest. Protests are a way for people to speak up together and say what they think is right or wrong. Sometimes, people protest because they want the government to do something different. The government is like the grown-ups who help run the country, like making rules and taking care of things for everyone. But there are always rules for how to do protests safely and nicely, so everyone stays happy and calm.

Who Are These Grown-Ups?

In Angola, there is a group of grown-ups. Some of them are from Angola, and some are from a far-away country called Russia. Russia is a very, very big country, much further away than your neighbouring town or even another African country. It's a long, long flight to get there! Someone has said that these Angolan and Russian grown-ups tried to start these 'protests' in a way that was not allowed. They are 'accused'. Being 'accused' means that someone thinks you did something, maybe something that breaks the rules. But these grown-ups, both the ones from Angola and the ones from Russia, say, "No! We didn't do that! We did not try to start protests in a wrong way." When they say "No! We didn't do that!" it means they 'deny' it. They are saying it is not true.

Going to Court to Find the Truth

So, when someone is accused of doing something, and they say they didn't do it, what happens next? They go to a special place called a court! This is where the judge helps everyone find out the truth. At the court, everyone gets a chance to speak. The people who are saying the grown-ups did something wrong will tell their side of the story. They will show any proof they have. Then, the Angolan and Russian grown-ups will also tell their side of the story. They will explain why they say they did not do what they are accused of. The judge will listen very carefully to everyone. The judge will listen to all the stories and look at all the proof. It's like when your teacher helps you and a friend solve a disagreement. The teacher listens to both sides to understand what really happened.

Why is This Important?

This is important because in every country, we have rules. These rules help everyone live together happily and safely. If someone thinks a rule has been broken, we have courts and judges to help us figure things out in a fair way. This helps keep peace in the country. It's very important that everyone gets a fair chance to tell their story. This is how justice works. Justice means making things fair and right for everyone.

Did You Know?

* Did you know Angola is famous for its diamonds and oil? These are very important things for its economy! * Did you know that in Africa, there are 54 different countries? Each one is unique and special! * Did you know that sometimes, people solve big problems by just talking and listening to each other very carefully? Using words is a powerful way to make peace! So, friends, that's the news from Angola. A group of people, from Angola and Russia, are going to court. Someone says they tried to start protests in a way that was not allowed. But they say, "No, we didn't!" Now, a judge will listen to everyone to find out the truth. It's all about fairness and following the rules in our wonderful world! Stay curious and keep listening to the news from Africa! See you next time!
summary: In Angola, a group of people from Angola and Russia are going to court because someone says they tried to start protests in a wrong way, but they say they did not do it, and a judge will help find the truth.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how countries use rules and courts to solve big disagreements peacefully, making sure everyone gets a fair chance to speak and find out what is true.

originalTitle: Moment rescuers find man alive under the rubble in Tehran
title: Big Happy News! Heroes Find a Man Alive!
content:
Jambo, my wonderful friends all across Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with some very, very special news. It's a story that makes our hearts feel warm and full of hope. It's about brave people and a miracle!

A Big Sad Day Turned Happy

Imagine a faraway place, a big city with tall buildings. One day, a very sad thing happened there. A big building, maybe someone's home, fell down. It crumbled into many, many pieces. This is what we call 'rubble'. Rubble is like a huge pile of broken bricks, concrete, and bits of wood. It's very dangerous and can hide things underneath. When a building falls, it's a very scary and sad time. People worry a lot about anyone who might be trapped inside. For many days, brave helpers worked very hard at this place. They are called 'rescuers'. Rescuers are like superheroes in real life!

Who Are These Amazing Rescuers?

Rescuers are people who are very strong and very kind. They help when there are big accidents or disasters. They wear special clothes and helmets to keep them safe. They have special tools too! Some tools can lift heavy things. Some tools can make small holes to look inside the rubble. They work day and night, even when it's dark or cold. Did you know that some rescuers even have special dogs? These dogs are very clever! They use their amazing noses to sniff out people who might be hidden. They are called 'search and rescue dogs'. Woof woof! These brave teams were looking for anyone who might still be under the big pile of broken bits. It was a very hard job, like looking for a tiny pebble in a mountain of rocks.

Searching for Hope

Every day, the rescuers listened very carefully. They used special listening tools. They tapped on the rubble, hoping someone would tap back. They knew that every second counted. They wanted to find people and help them get to safety. Their hearts were full of hope and determination. Many people from the community also came to help. They brought water and food for the rescuers. They prayed and hoped for good news. It shows how people come together in difficult times, just like in our own communities. They never gave up. Even when it seemed like a very, very long time, they kept digging, listening, and hoping.

The Amazing Moment!

And then, my friends, something truly, truly wonderful happened! After many hours, many days of hard work, the rescuers heard something. A small sound! It was a person! They worked even faster, but very carefully. They moved the big pieces of rubble away, one by one. Their hearts were beating fast with excitement and nervousness. They shouted, "Hello! Can you hear us?" And guess what? A voice answered back! It was a man! Can you imagine the happiness? The rescuers cheered! They were so, so happy. They had found a man alive under all that heavy rubble. It was like finding a shining star in the darkest night. Everyone felt so much joy. All their hard work had paid off.

Safe at Last!

The rescuers were super careful to pull the man out. They made sure he was safe and sound. He was tired, but he was alive! They quickly gave him some water and checked if he was hurt. Then, he was taken to a safe place where doctors and nurses could look after him. He was safe at last! This story reminds us that even after very sad events, there can be good news. It reminds us never to give up hope. It shows us how strong people can be.

Why This Story Makes Us Happy

This story is important because it shows us many good things. It shows us how brave people are. The rescuers worked so hard, for so long, to save a life. They are true heroes! It also shows us the power of hope. Even when things look very bad, if we keep hoping and trying, good things can happen. And it shows us how important it is to help each other. When someone is in trouble, it's good to lend a hand, just like the community helped the rescuers. Did you know that people can sometimes survive under rubble for many days? This is because they might find small pockets of air or water. It's truly amazing how strong the human body can be!

Let's Be Safe and Strong!

This story also reminds us about safety. It's important for our homes and schools to be strong and safe. We should always know what to do in case of an emergency, like an earthquake or a fire. Always listen to your parents and teachers about safety rules. They want to keep you safe and sound.

Heroes Everywhere!

So, my friends, remember this story of the brave rescuers and the man they saved. It tells us that even in big, scary situations, there are always heroes. They might not wear capes, but they do amazing, life-saving work. And remember, you can be a hero too! By being kind, helping your friends, listening to your elders, and staying safe, you can make the world a better place, just like those amazing rescuers. Keep shining, Africa!
summary: Brave rescuers found a man alive under a big pile of broken building pieces after a sad accident, showing us the power of hope and helping each other.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even after sad things happen, brave people can help and save lives, giving everyone hope and showing how important it is to be kind and work together.

originalTitle: UN experts urge Israel to free Gaza doctor amid reports of ‘severe torture’
title: A Special Doctor Needs Our Help!
content:
Hello Friends! Meet Your Reporter!

Hello, my amazing friends! It's your friendly news reporter bringing you exciting stories from all over our big continent, Africa, and sometimes even from other parts of the world! Today, we have a very important story about a doctor. Doctors are super special people, aren't they? They help us feel better when we are sick or when we get a little booboo. Imagine a world without doctors! It would be very tricky to stay healthy.

Meet Dr. Hussam – A Very Special Doctor

Today, we are talking about a wonderful doctor named Dr. Hussam Abu Safia. He lives in a place called Gaza, which is far away from many of us. Dr. Hussam is just like the doctors you see at your clinic or hospital. He works hard every day to help people who are not feeling well. He makes sure little children get their medicines and that grown-ups feel strong again. Doctors like him are heroes! They spend many years learning how our bodies work. They learn how to fix us when we are broken or sick.

What Do Doctors Do?

Did you know that doctors have many important jobs? They listen to your heart with a stethoscope. That's a special tool that helps them hear your heartbeat! They might look at your throat to check for a cough or a cold. Doctors also give you special shots, called vaccines, to help you stay strong and not get sick from bad germs. They teach us how to eat healthy foods and how to play safely. Doctors are like health detectives, figuring out what makes us sick and how to make us better. They want everyone to be happy and healthy!

Why Doctors Need Our Help Too

Now, sometimes even our heroes, like doctors, need help themselves. Our story today is about Dr. Hussam, who is in a bit of trouble. He is in a place where he cannot go home, and he is not getting the medical help he needs. Imagine you have a tummy ache, and no one gives you medicine. Or you fall and scrape your knee, but no one puts a bandage on it. That wouldn't be fair, would it? Everyone, especially doctors who help so many, deserves to be cared for when they are sick.

Who Are the Big Helpers? (UN Experts)

Some very kind and important people from all over the world are trying to help Dr. Hussam. These special people are called "UN experts." UN stands for United Nations. They are like a big team of grown-ups who work together to make the world a better and fairer place for everyone. They speak up for people who are not being treated fairly. When they heard that Dr. Hussam was not getting his medicine or help, they said, "This is not right! He needs help!" They want to make sure Dr. Hussam is safe and healthy, just like he helps his own patients.

What Happens When Doctors Are Not Well?

It's very important for doctors to be healthy and safe. If a doctor is sick or hurt and cannot get help, then they cannot help others! Think about it: if your teacher gets sick, it's harder to learn. If the bus driver is not well, it's harder to get to school. In the same way, if a doctor is unwell, many other people might not get the help they need. This is why the big helpers, the UN experts, are so worried about Dr. Hussam. They want him to be free and get all the care he needs so he can go back to helping people.

Being Fair to Everyone

Did you know that every single person in the world has rights? Rights are like special rules that say what you should have and what people should do for you. One of these important rights is to get help when you are sick. It doesn't matter who you are, where you come from, or what job you do. Everyone deserves to be treated kindly and to get good medical care if they need it. This is what fairness means. It's like sharing your toys or making sure everyone gets a turn on the swing. The UN experts are trying to make sure Dr. Hussam's rights are respected. They believe he should be free and get proper medical care.

Let's All Be Kind and Healthy!

So, what can we learn from this story, my friends? We learn that doctors are super important, and they need our care and respect too. We learn that it's important to speak up when someone is not being treated fairly. And we learn that being healthy is a wonderful gift. Remember to wash your hands, eat your yummy vegetables, and play outside! And always be kind to everyone, because everyone deserves care and fairness. Let's hope Dr. Hussam gets the help he needs very soon so he can continue his important work!
summary: Kind helpers from around the world are asking for a special doctor named Dr. Hussam to get the medical help he needs because everyone deserves care when they are sick.
whyMatters: It's important because doctors help us stay healthy, and they also need to be safe and get help if they are sick, just like everyone else, so they can keep helping others.
